findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 34. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: The introductory paragraph and report outline appear twice in the HTML: once in the initial section before the TOC navigation, and again implicitly through the full body content. The opening memo text (To/From/Subject, introductory paragraph, and outline) appears both in the header/intro area and then again in the main body, duplicating the content that appeared only once in the PDF.
findings.evidence: The paragraph 'On March 22, 2016, I served as meeting officer...' and the four-item outline (I-IV) appear both above the <nav> element and then again as part of the main body content sections. In the PDF, this content appears only once on page 1.

findings.description: In the Attachments section (Section IV), the HTML lists items V and VI with swapped descriptions compared to the PDF page 4 body text references.
findings.evidence: PDF page 4 body text references: 'Attachment V-Supporting Documentation' and 'Attachment VI-USPS Comment'. PDF page 5 attachment list: 'V. Written Public Comments Received' and 'VI. Supporting documentation received during public hearing'. The HTML attachment list matches page 5 (V = Written Public Comments Received, VI = Supporting documentation), which is faithful. However, the body text on page 4 references 'Attachment V-Supporting Documentation' for hand-submitted comments and 'Attachment VI-USPS Comment' for USPS letters - this inconsistency exists in the source PDF itself.
